bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening since there is no time pressure yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, with concessions only on issueD and issueE, so I will keep issueD as valueB since it is equally best for me while holding firm on the other high-value issues.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early at t=0.02 and there is no deadline pressure to concede. The opponent’s second offer is worse for me than their opening offer, so they are not showing meaningful concession; with no messages to weigh, I will keep a strong bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous offer.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid because it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), so there is little reason to concede much yet. The opponent’s latest valid offer improved my utility to about 0.60 by conceding on issueA while keeping weak values on issueB, issueC, and issueF; I will keep firm on my high-weight issues and choose issueD:valueB, which they have repeatedly offered and is equally best for me.

I am targeting my maximum-utility bid of 1.00 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to make substantial concessions yet. The opponent briefly improved to a bid worth about 0.60 to me at t=0.04 but then fell back to about 0.44, so their behavior does not show steady concession; they seem to push hard for valueC on issueB, issueC, and issueF. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by choosing issueD: valueA, which is equally good for me as valueB.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is little need to concede on high-weight issues. The opponent has improved their offers for me on issueA and issueD but is still holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF at low values, so I will keep my key preferences while varying issueD to avoid simply repeating my previous bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.10, but I am making a small concession on the low-weight issueF to avoid simply repeating my prior position. The opponent’s offers strongly favor issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC while conceding sometimes on issueA and issueD, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueB and issueC and use issueF as the first tradeoff.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid of about 0.964, conceding only on issueF while using issueD:valueB since it is equally valuable to me as valueA. Time is still early at t=0.12, so I should not make major concessions yet; the opponent’s latest offer improved on issueB and keeps issueE favorable, but remains weak for me on issueA, issueC, and issueF.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.92 because it is early in the negotiation, so there is no need for deep concessions yet. The opponent’s valid offers consistently keep issueE at valueA and issueF at valueC, and they have recently conceded on issueC/issueD, so I concede the low-weight issueF while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early in the negotiation and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s latest valid offer has improved substantially for me on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE, but they continue to push issueB: valueC and issueF: valueC, so I concede on the lower-weight issueF while holding firm on high-weight issueB.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early in the negotiation at t=0.18, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent consistently wants issueF: valueC and accepts issueE: valueA, while they have shown flexibility on issueA, issueC, and issueD; I keep my high-weight preferences on issueA, issueB, and issueC while choosing issueD: valueA to avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to concede on my most important issues like issueB and issueC. The opponent’s offers consistently give issueE=valueA and issueF=valueC, and their latest offer moved issueA to valueB and issueD to valueB, so I concede on issueA slightly and keep issueF at their apparent preference while holding firm elsewhere.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.22 and the opponent’s latest offer is very low for me, especially on issueB, issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF. The opponent repeatedly signals preference for issueF:valueC and often offers issueE:valueA and issueD:valueB, so I concede on low-weight issueF while holding firm on the higher-weight issues that matter most to me.

I am targeting a high but slightly concessive utility around 0.90, since the negotiation is still early-mid at t=0.24 and there is no need to drop to the opponent’s last 0.76-utility offer yet. The opponent has consistently pushed issueF=valueC and recently accepted/gave favorable values on issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE, so I keep those strong for me while conceding issueF and slightly softening issueA to valueB.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.90 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26, so there is no need for a large concession yet. The opponent has shown willingness to accept issueC:valueA, issueE:valueA, and issueF:valueC, while varying issueD; I concede the low-weight issueF and offer issueD:valueA to align with their latest bid, while holding firmer on higher-value issues.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility, since it is still early-mid negotiation and the opponent has only occasionally made offers that are reasonably good for me. Their bids suggest willingness on issueB=valueB, issueC=valueA, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA at times, while they keep pushing issueF=valueC, so I concede there and keep my higher-weight issues strong.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92–0.96 because it is still only t=0.30, but the opponent’s latest valid offer is already very favorable to me at about 0.92 utility. Their bidding shows strong insistence on issueF=valueC while conceding substantially on issueA, issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE, so I will preserve their signaled position on issueF to improve agreement chances while holding my high-value issues.
